rumors: Companions can grow into heroes if you keep them alive.
Blessed water cleanses chaos. Cursed water spreads it.
A ward buys time, but attacking from it scratches it away.
Chaos vents reward curiosity with mutations.
Pilgrims know the paths between altars and chaos vents.
The Root-Father's patience outlasts every blade; the Ember Mother's hunger outlasts every hearth.
Trolls knit their wounds shut between blows. Put them down before they mend.
Wizards and dragons spit ruin from afar. Close the distance, or carry a wand.
A cornered beast forgets its fear. Leave the wounded a road and they will take it.
Deep-forged steel is marked: a vicious edge or masterwork plate is worth the weight.
Corruption is not a punishment. It is a bargain, and the price comes due in the dark.
Offer the dead on an altar and your god remembers. Hoard them and they only rot.
The Veiled Witness keeps no temples, only secrets. Pray in the dust and listen.
Deeper is richer, and deeper is hungrier. Carry rations, or carry regrets.
They say the amulet that frees you sleeps in the lowest dark, kept by the worst of it.
